rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee/eq/const in component type annexes for interface behavior; use implementation annexes only for assign clauses tied to subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible feature names or explicitly declared constants/variables in AGREE expressions; never reference AADL properties directly.
generic_rules: Contracts should express observable interface behavior using named ports, not internal implementation details.
Boolean logic in guarantees should be grounded in explicit input-output relations, not abstract conditions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; with semicolons after each declaration.
Use const or eq to define derived values or logical conditions before referencing them in assume/guarantee.
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions without explicit typed port or constant mapping.
Avoid placing implementation-level assignments in component type annexes.
